steps.title: Translate the relationship to an equation
steps.content: Weight equals rate times volume, so $x = 160V$, where $x$ is weight in pounds and $V$ is volume in cubic feet.

steps.title: Apply the volume inequality
steps.content:
The volume is constrained: $12.7 \lt V \lt 15.7$. Multiply all parts by $160$ (positive, so inequality direction is preserved):
$160(12.7) \lt 160V \lt 160(15.7)$, which gives $160(12.7) \lt x \lt 160(15.7)$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm C
steps.content:
• Choice A — $160 - 15.7 \lt x \lt 160 - 12.7$: Uses subtraction instead of multiplication; weight doesn’t subtract from rate. ✗
  • Choice B — $160 + 12.7 \lt x \lt 160 + 15.7$: Uses addition instead of multiplication; incorrect operation. ✗
  • Choice C — $160(12.7) \lt x \lt 160(15.7)$: Multiplying the bounding volumes by 160 lb/ft³ gives the weight bounds directly. ✓
  • Choice D — $\frac{160}{15.7} \lt x \lt \frac{160}{12.7}$: Division inverts the relationship and also reverses the bounds incorrectly. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
